mentions.note: Down 23% YTD but Cramer is buyer not seller. CEO Renee Haas doing real good job despite stock weakness.
Stock in freefall with no clear catalyst; hold existing position and wait for CEO Renee Haase interview to clarify SoftBank stake situation and valuation reset.
Very interesting; Renee Huang (CEO) got shortage coming right; great buy. CEO bet validated by chip shortage dynamics.
Caller Nick: down at 152 from highs. In every device; Rene Haas doing terrific job. Don't exit despite pain.
CPU demand surging in agentic AI era; data centers becoming largest business; 50% power efficiency advantage; 22 million developer flywheel; stock stuck but fundamentals accelerating.
Benefits from AI agents adopting more agile CPUs alongside Nvidia GPUs. Tighter Nvidia partnership expected long-term.

Arm Neoverse CPU (2x performance at same power vs. X86) transforms company from licensor to chip maker for agentic AI workloads. Meta first major customer; $15B+ annual CPU sales target within 5 years. Stock up 25% YTD; massive long-term runway from $50B IPO valuation to $150B current market cap.
Prefer over Qualcomm; making good steps vs QCOM missteps.
CPU design company; recently added to Charitable Trust; stock at $21 post-call. Rene Haas CEO expects strong earnings. Cramer: 'Type of thing you got to be in.' Good business with momentum.
Best capital expenditure play alongside Lam Research and Applied Materials. Downgraded to buy-on-weakness after 71% March-April surge; trim on parabolic moves.
Parabolic move threatening to touch the sun; trust owns for Investing Club; healthy correction needed.
Competitor position benefiting from data center buildout; stock going parabolic—Cramer doesn't buy parabolas; position being built ahead of May 6 earnings but stock has gotten away.
Reports after close Wednesday. Designs CPU architecture for most chips. Often overlooked that ARM makes CPUs itself. Stock could romp; went big today then reversed—watch carefully.
Semiconductor powering AI; part of cohort displacing software as market leader; not specifically recommended.
Pivoting from licensing to manufacturing own CPUs; massive tailwind from AI agents needing advanced processors; CEO Rene Haas bullish and straight with investors.
